title: Provenance model + countable assertion (administered-item) unit
context: The project's quality bar requires 100% provenance on every assertion — each CDE, vocabulary binding, crosswalk row, governance clause, and regulatory claim. To make this mechanically checkable by CI, the project needs a defined provenance model with a named countable unit (the administered item, per ISO/IEC 11179 discipline). Without this model, the provenance-completeness linter (ci-001) cannot be built. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-gov-001.
objective: Define the provenance model for the project: (1) name and describe the countable assertion unit (the administered item, per ISO/IEC 11179 discipline applied — not text copied), (2) specify the required provenance record fields for every administered item (source name, identifier/citation, retrieval date, URL, verified licence/legal status), and (3) define staleness semantics with `lastVerified` and `validUntil` fields and the fail-safe auto-flag/withhold behaviour for stale HIGH-tier claims.
acceptanceCriteria: The administered item is named as the countable assertion unit; the definition aligns with ISO/IEC 11179 metadata-registry discipline without reproducing copyrighted standard text.
Required provenance record fields specified: source name, identifier/citation, retrieval date, URL, and verified licence/legal status.
Staleness fields defined: `lastVerified` (date) and `validUntil` (date), with per-source-type review intervals (e.g., ontologies on major release; regulatory mapping on short cadence).

Fail-safe staleness behaviour documented: a binding or claim past `validUntil` is auto-flagged; HIGH-tier regulatory claims past `validUntil` are withheld from publication until re-verified and re-signed-off.
Provenance model covers all administered-item classes: CDEs, vocabulary bindings, crosswalk rows, governance clauses, and regulatory claims.
Published as a human-readable governance document committed via PR, referenced in the project constitution and the CI scaffold.
output: A `governance/provenance-model.md` document defining the administered-item unit, provenance record fields, and staleness semantics, committed via PR.
